title: Isodon kangtingensis
taxonomy: Isodon kangtingensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Isodon, which is a group of plants commonly known as Chinese lavender or isodon. The plant's classification is further divided into the subfamily Lamioideae, which includes many species of mint and related plants. The species Isodon kangtingensis is native to China, where it grows in mountainous regions. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships to other plants within the Lamiaceae family.

Isodon kangtingensis has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its antifungal and antibacterial properties. intro: Isodon kangtingensis, also known as Kangting Isodon, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its distinctive foliage and flowering habits. Studies have shown that this plant possesses various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory disorders and promote overall well-being.
